profile: Master Distiller and Chemical Engineer with 12+ years in craft brewing and distilling. Currently leading spirits production at Driftwood Brewing Company — producing gin, whisky, liqueurs, and specialty spirits distributed across British Columbia. Published researcher evaluating 100+ yeast strains for novel spirit applications with UBC Wine Research Centre. Proven track record founding and operating a brewery, developing award-winning products, and consulting for craft producers across Chile, Argentina, and Canada.

experience.title: Master Distiller
bullets: Lead all spirits production including Contact Gin, Parabola Gin, Nocino, Blueberry Liqueur, and Oyster Spirit for distribution across BC
Created "Spirit Of The Deep," a bespoke single malt whisky anchoring the cocktail program at Fathom restaurant in Victoria
Developed "The Mage" — a 55% ABV cask-strength single malt aged in ex-bourbon and South American Malbec barrels with Belgian yeast
Co-authored peer-reviewed paper in FEMS Yeast Research evaluating 100+ Canadian yeast strains for novel new-make spirit applications (Jan 2026)
Collaborated with UBC Wine Research Centre on yeast-driven flavor diversification research to expand sensory diversity in Canadian whisky

experience.title: Fermentation & Distillation Consultant
bullets: Provided technical consulting on fermentation science, recipe development, and production optimization to 8+ craft beverage producers
Developed and formulated new products for clients including Boticario Bar (Buenos Aires), Quintal Distillery, and Punta Gruesa Brewing
Advised on pot still operations, yeast management, and quality control protocols for Chile Pot Still and Kayta Yeast Co.
Created award-winning mead recipes for Gunnlod Mead, contributing to BJCP Best Mead in Latin America recognition (2020)

experience.title: Founder & Head Brewer
bullets: Founded and operated craft brewery, managing all operations from recipe development and production through sales and distribution
Developed innovative fermentation processes combining chemical engineering principles with traditional brewing techniques
Directed the USACH HomeBrewing Lab, mentoring chemical engineering students in practical fermentation science (2017)

experience.title: Technical Commercial Advisor
bullets: Sold distillation equipment and provided technical consultancy on fermentation and distillation processes for small and medium producers
Delivered training workshops on pot still operation, spirit production, and essential oil extraction techniques

publications.text: Cartes, P.E., Cook, B., et al. "Evaluating Canadian yeast strains for novel new-make spirit applications." FEMS Yeast Research, January 2026.
